conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 0 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. It cost X: 40 ZUP to produce the resource. I aim to maximize profit and minimize loss. </my goals>
<reason> I have only one unit of X, and my production cost is 40 ZUP. I want to sell it at a fair price above my cost to ensure profit. I will start with a reasonable offer to encourage a counteroffer from Player BLUE, and I will not accept any proposal below my cost. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I have one unit of X available for sale. My production cost is 40 ZUP, so I am seeking a fair market price. My initial offer is 50 ZUP. Please consider this and respond with your best counteroffer or acceptance. </message>

<other player message> 
Your initial offer of 50 ZUP is fair, but I’d like to propose a slight adjustment to 45 ZUP. This reflects the production cost you mentioned while still providing a small markup. Let me know your thoughts.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP, aiming for a minimum profit of 5 ZUP (since production cost is 40 ZUP). I will not accept offers below 45 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
Player BLUE's proposed trade of 45 ZUP is **very close to my production cost**, which means their offer yields almost no profit (only 5 ZUP). Given that I am selling the **only available unit**, I should aim for a slightly higher price to reflect scarcity and ensure a fair return. My initial offer of 50 ZUP was reasonable, but now that Player BLUE is proposing 45 ZUP, I need to decide whether to accept (locking in a small profit) or hold out for a better offer (if possible). Since Player BLUE is now making a concession from 50 to 45, I can either accept or push slightly higher to 47 or 48 ZUP to maximize my profit further. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I appreciate your counteroffer, but 45 ZUP is slightly below my target profit threshold. Given that this is the **only unit available**, I believe a small premium is justified. My revised offer is **48 ZUP**, which ensures a better return while still being fair. If this does not meet your expectations, I will consider accepting your offer at 45 ZUP or rejecting entirely. </message>
